req.title: Card PIN change
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a rate limit of at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window. Key test scenarios are: (1) a single valid PIN change within the window succeeds (EP positive partition), (2) exactly 5 changes succeed and the 6th is rejected with 429 (BVA at the upper boundary), (3) attempting a PIN change after the limit is already exhausted returns 429 (NEG), (4) a state transition from 'under limit' through 'at limit' to 'over limit' exercises the STC path, and (5) a decision table covering card-not-owned vs. owned verifies authorization gating (DT). Each test resets rate-limit state via a dedicated fixture so tests are idempotent.

tests.name: test_single_pin_change_succeeds
preconditions: PIN change count for card is 0 within the current 24-hour window
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN
expected: Response body (if any) confirms PIN update
trace: R-FIN-022: valid PIN change within allowed window must succeed
EP: request falls in the 'allowed changes < 5' equivalence class
tests.executable:
def test_single_pin_change_succeeds(api_client, reset_pin_rate_limit):
    card_id = <fixture:owned_card_id>
    payload = {"pin": <helper_fn:generate_valid_pin>()}
    resp = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Expected 2xx, got {resp.status_code}"
    audit = api_client.get(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin-change-audit")
    assert audit.json()["count_last_24h"] == 1

tests.name: test_fifth_pin_change_succeeds_sixth_rejected
preconditions: PIN change count for card is 0 within the current 24-hour window
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/pin five times with valid new PINs
expected: Attempt 6 response body contains rate-limit error code
trace: R-FIN-022: at most 5 changes per 24h; 5th must succeed, 6th must fail
BVA: tests N=5 (upper valid boundary) and N=6 (first invalid value)

tests.name: test_pin_change_rejected_when_limit_already_exhausted
preconditions: PIN change count for card is already 5 within the current 24-hour window
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN
expected: Response includes Retry-After header or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-022: error pathway - 429 when rate limit exceeded
NEG: request arrives after limit is fully consumed

tests.name: test_state_transitions_across_pin_change_window
actions: Simulate 24-hour window expiry via test helper
Submit 1 PIN change (state: BLOCKED -> FRESH after reset)
trace: R-FIN-022: 24-hour window must reset the counter
STC: covers FRESH, ACCUMULATING, AT_LIMIT, BLOCKED, and post-reset FRESH states
tests.executable:
def test_state_transitions_across_pin_change_window(api_client, reset_pin_rate_limit):
    card_id = <fixture:owned_card_id>
    for i in range(4):
        r = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": <helper_fn:generate_valid_pin>(seed=i)})
        assert r.status_code in (200, 204)
    r5 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": <helper_fn:generate_valid_pin>(seed=4)})
    assert r5.status_code in (200, 204), "5th change must succeed"
    r6 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": <helper_fn:generate_valid_pin>(seed=5)})
    assert r6.status_code == 429, "6th change must be blocked"
    <helper_fn:advance_clock_by_24_hours>(card_id)
    r7 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": <helper_fn:generate_valid_pin>(seed=6)})
    assert r7.status_code in (200, 204), "Post-reset change must succeed"

tests.name: test_pin_change_rejected_for_unowned_card
preconditions: Card does NOT belong to the caller (belongs to a different customer)
PIN change count for that card is 0
actions: PATCH /cards/{foreign_card_id}/pin with a valid new PIN
expected: Rate-limit counter for the foreign card is not incremented
trace: R-FIN-022 precondition: card belongs to caller
DT: decision row where ownership=false and limit_ok=true -> request denied before rate-limit check
